title: Sakkarai Pongal

subs.name: chana-dal (soaked 30 min)
subs.notes: gives a nuttier, chewier texture; cook 2 extra whistles to ensure it softens

ingredients.name: jaggery (vellam), grated or broken
visual_checks: palm jaggery (karupatti) gives a deeper, smokier sweetness than cane jaggery
ingredients.notes: palm jaggery (karupatti) gives a deeper, smokier sweetness than cane jaggery

subs.name: brown sugar (reduce to 0.75 cup)
subs.notes: lacks the complex caramel depth of jaggery; add a pinch of cardamom extra to compensate

ingredients.name: ghee
visual_checks: use generously — this is a festive dish
ingredients.notes: use generously — this is a festive dish

subs.name: coconut oil (3 tbsp)
subs.notes: dairy-free version; gives a coconutty note that actually complements the jaggery well

ingredients.name: grated coconut
visual_checks: folded in at the end; traditional in some families
ingredients.notes: folded in at the end; traditional in some families

steps.text: Dry-roast the moong dal in the pressure cooker base for 1–2 minutes on medium flame until it smells nutty. Add washed rice, 4 cups water and a tiny pinch of salt. Pressure cook for 5 whistles on medium-high flame until the rice and dal are very soft, slightly overcooked and beginning to meld together. Let pressure release naturally.

While the pongal cooks, make the jaggery syrup: dissolve grated jaggery in 0.5 cup water in a saucepan on low flame, stirring until fully melted. Strain through a sieve to remove any grit. The syrup should be thin and dark.
Mash the cooked rice-dal mixture well with the back of a ladle — you want it smooth and creamy with no separate grains visible. If it is stiff, add a splash of hot water.

Pour the strained jaggery syrup into the mashed rice mixture and stir on medium-low flame. Cook for 3–4 minutes, stirring constantly, until the jaggery is absorbed and the pongal turns glossy and a deep caramel colour.
Heat ghee in a small pan and fry cashews until golden, then add raisins until they puff. Pour the entire contents — ghee, cashews and raisins — over the pongal. Add crushed cardamom and fold through.
Taste for sweetness — add more jaggery syrup if needed. Fold in grated coconut if using. Serve warm. Sakkarai pongal thickens as it cools; reheat with a splash of water and a small knob of ghee.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: jaggery → brown sugar (reduce to 0.75 cup) (lacks the complex caramel depth of jaggery; add a pinch of cardamom extra to compensate); ghee → coconut oil (3 tbsp) (dairy-free version; gives a coconutty note that actually complements the jaggery well); moong-dal → chana-dal (soaked 30 min) (gives a nuttier, chewier texture; cook 2 extra whistles to ensure it softens)
